title: Sudden Choking - Foreign Body Aspiration
chief_complaint: 2-year-old with acute onset coughing and choking while eating peanuts. Now has persistent cough, unilateral wheezing on right side, and decreased breath sounds right lung.

physical_exam.chest: Decreased breath sounds right side, expiratory wheezing right, trachea slightly deviated to right

hidden_data.cxr_inspiratory: Hyperinflation of right lung on expiratory film, mediastinal shift to left on inspiration
hidden_data.bronchoscopy: Peanut fragment removed from right main bronchus

key_learning_points: Sudden onset choking + unilateral wheeze = foreign body until proven
Expiratory CXR shows air trapping on affected side

patient_responses.choking: He was eating peanuts and suddenly started coughing and couldn't breathe
patient_responses.now: He's still coughing and making a whistling sound
patient_responses.color: He looked blue for a moment

feedback.correct_message: Excellent! Foreign body aspiration is a pediatric emergency.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is foreign body aspiration. The history + unilateral findings are classic.

cxr_inspiratory.comment: Shows air trapping on affected side.

feedback.attending_note: Foreign body aspiration: Rigid bronchoscopy for removal. Do NOT do blind finger sweep. If complete obstruction with cyanosis: back blows/chest thrusts for infants, Heimlich for >1 year. Prevention: avoid peanuts and small objects in children <3 years.
